# A meta-analysis framework for population PK/PD

Aggregate data modelling · nlmixr2 / rxode2

Fit one unified population model to what the literature reports —
aggregate summary data or previously published PK/PD models — without
individual patient data.

[![R-CMD-check](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/LeidenPharmacology/admixr2/R-CMD-check.yaml?style=flat-square&label=R-CMD-check)](https://github.com/LeidenPharmacology/admixr2/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![CRAN](https://img.shields.io/cran/v/admixr2?style=flat-square)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=admixr2)
[![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/LeidenPharmacology/admixr2?style=flat-square&label=coverage)](https://app.codecov.io/gh/LeidenPharmacology/admixr2)
[![Lifecycle:
stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/lifecycle-stable-4A6B5D?style=flat-square)](https://lifecycle.r-lib.org/articles/stages.html#stable)
[![DOI](https://img.shields.io/badge/DOI-10.1007%2Fs10928--025--10011--w-1F4E79?style=flat-square)](https://doi.org/10.1007/s10928-025-10011-w)

[Get
started](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/admixr2.md)
[GitHub](https://github.com/LeidenPharmacology/admixr2)

\> `install.packages("admixr2")`

meta-analysis.R

``` adm-code
# Fit a model to published means + covariances
fit <- nlmixr2(model, admData(), est = "admc",
  control = admControl(studies = list(
    trial_A = list(E = Ea, V = Va, n = 120L,
                   times = c(1, 2, 4, 8, 24),
                   ev = et(amt = 100)))))

plot(fit)   # observed mean vs prediction, ±1 SD
```

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

observed study mean ± SD  vs  model prediction

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMTkiIGhlaWdodD0iMTkiIHZpZXdib3g9IjAgMCAyNCAyNCIgZmlsbD0ibm9uZSIgc3Ryb2tlPSJjdXJyZW50Q29sb3IiIHN0cm9rZS13aWR0aD0iMS41Ij48cmVjdCB4PSI0IiB5PSIzIiB3aWR0aD0iMTYiIGhlaWdodD0iMTgiIHJ4PSIyIiAvPjxwYXRoIGQ9Ik04IDhoOE04IDEyaDhNOCAxNmg1IiBzdHJva2UtbGluZWNhcD0icm91bmQiIC8+PC9zdmc+)

**Aggregate data**published means, error bars & covariances

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

**PK/PD models**previously published population models

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMjgiIGhlaWdodD0iMjgiIHZpZXdib3g9IjAgMCAyNCAyNCIgZmlsbD0ibm9uZSIgc3Ryb2tlPSJjdXJyZW50Q29sb3IiIHN0cm9rZS13aWR0aD0iMS42Ij48cGF0aCBkPSJNNCAxMmgxNU0xNCA2bDYgNi02IDYiIHN0cm9rZS1saW5lY2FwPSJyb3VuZCIgc3Ryb2tlLWxpbmVqb2luPSJyb3VuZCIgLz48L3N2Zz4=)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

#### Aggregate data modelling

One likelihood over the summary-level information — a meta-analysis
across studies.

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMjgiIGhlaWdodD0iMjgiIHZpZXdib3g9IjAgMCAyNCAyNCIgZmlsbD0ibm9uZSIgc3Ryb2tlPSJjdXJyZW50Q29sb3IiIHN0cm9rZS13aWR0aD0iMS42Ij48cGF0aCBkPSJNNCAxMmgxNU0xNCA2bDYgNi02IDYiIHN0cm9rZS1saW5lY2FwPSJyb3VuZCIgc3Ryb2tlLWxpbmVqb2luPSJyb3VuZCIgLz48L3N2Zz4=)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

#### Unified population model

One fit with interpretable fixed, random and covariate effects.

Four estimators, one interface

## Choose the method that fits your problem

Every backend plugs into `nlmixr2(…, est=)` with analytical gradients.
Compare them side by side in the [estimator
vignette](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/estimator-comparison.md).

adfo

### First-order

Analytical first-order linearisation — deterministic, no Monte-Carlo
draws. Typically the fastest backend.

admc

### Monte Carlo

Simulation of the aggregate likelihood, with analytical
common-random-number gradients. Accuracy improves with the number of
draws.

adgh

### Gauss–Hermite

Deterministic quadrature over the random effects — noise-free, with an
exact gradient. Most efficient when the random-effect dimension is
small.

adirmc

### Iterative RMC

Iterative Reweighting Monte Carlo — reweights simulated draws across
iterations, with a kappa correction for non-linear models.

Documentation

## Guides, grouped by what you’re doing

Nine worked vignettes, from your first fit to publication-grade
diagnostics.

### Getting started

- [Introduction to
  admixr2](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/admixr2.md)

  n](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/aggregate-data.md)
- [Simulating data & using published
  models](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/datagen.md)

### Meta-analysis across studies

- [Multiple
  studies](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/multiple-studies.md)
- [Several outputs (plasma +
  brain)](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/multi-compartment.md)
- [PD and PK/PD
  data](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/pkpd.md)

### Estimation & diagnostics

- [Comparing the
  estimators](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/estimator-comparison.md)
- [Advanced
  usage](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/advanced.md)
- [Diagnostic
  plots](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/diagnostic-plots.md)

##### How to cite

van de Beek H., Välitalo P.A.J., Zwep L.B., van Hasselt J.G.C. (2025).
*admixr2: Aggregate Data Modelling.* Journal of Pharmacokinetics and
Pharmacodynamics.
[doi:10.1007/s10928-025-10011-w](https://doi.org/10.1007/s10928-025-10011-w)

Developed at Leiden University

started](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/admixr2.md)[Reference](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/index.md)[Changelog](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/news/index.md)

[nlmixr2](https://nlmixr2.org/)[rxode2](https://cran.r-project.org/package=rxode2)[GitHub](https://github.com/LeidenPharmacology/admixr2)

# Package index

## Fitting

Main entry point and data helper.

- [`admData()`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/admData.md)
  : Dummy data frame for nlmixr2 dispatch
- [`nlmixr2Est(`*`<adfo>`*`)`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/nlmixr2Est.adfo.md)
  : Fit an aggregate data model via First-Order (FO) approximation
- [`nlmixr2Est(`*`<admc>`*`)`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/nlmixr2Est.admc.md)
  : Fit an aggregate data model via Monte Carlo (admc estimator)
- [`nlmixr2Est(`*`<adgh>`*`)`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/nlmixr2Est.adgh.md)
  : Fit an aggregate data model via Gauss-Hermite quadrature
- [`nlmixr2Est(`*`<adirmc>`*`)`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/nlmixr2Est.adirmc.md)
  : Fit an aggregate data model via Iterative Reweighting MC (adirmc
  estimator)

## Simulation

Generate aggregate data from models for simulation studies.

- [`datagen()`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/datagen.md)
  : Generate aggregate study data from (possibly different)
  pharmacometric models

- [`datagenControl()`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/datagenControl.md)
  :

  Control parameters for
  [`datagen()`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/datagen.md)

## Control objects

Configuration for each estimator.

- [`adfoControl()`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/adfoControl.md)
  : Control settings for the FO (First-Order) estimator
- [`admControl()`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/admControl.md)
  : Control settings for the ADM estimator
- [`adghControl()`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/adghControl.md)
  : Control settings for the Gauss-Hermite (GH) quadrature estimator
- [`adirmcControl()`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/adirmcControl.md)
  : Control settings for the IRMC estimator
- [`admStopWorkers()`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/admStopWorkers.md)
  : Stop parallel workers

## Methods

S3 methods for fit objects.

- [`print(`*`<admFit>`*`)`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/print.admFit.md)
  : Print method for admFit objects
- [`plot(`*`<admFit>`*`)`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/plot.admFit.md)
  : Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit

## Data

Example datasets.

- [`examplomycin`](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/reference/examplomycin.md)
  : Examplomycin dataset

# Articles

### Blog

- [The mathematics of aggregate data
  modelling](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/introducing-admixr2.md):

  Aggregate data modelling has a long history in pharmacometrics. This
  post lays out the mathematical foundations of admixr2: the aggregate
  likelihood, four estimators for evaluating it, and the algorithmic
  innovations — particularly the common-random-numbers analytical
  gradient and the analytical IRMC inner gradient — that make
  gradient-based optimisation both fast and reliable.

### Getting started

Install admixr2 and fit your first model to aggregate data.

- [Getting started with
  admixr2](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/admixr2.md):

### Inputs — data & models

The two ways to feed a meta-analysis — digitized study summaries, or
previously published PK/PD models.

- [From a published figure to E, V and
  n](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/aggregate-data.md):
- [Generating aggregate data with
  datagen](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/datagen.md):

### Meta-analysis across studies

Combine evidence across sources — several studies, several observed
outputs, and PD / PK-PD endpoints.

- [Multiple
  studies](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/multiple-studies.md):
- [Several observed compartments (plasma and
  brain)](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/multi-compartment.md):
- [PD and PK/PD
  data](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/pkpd.md):

### Estimation & diagnostics

Choose and tune an estimator — gradients, restarts, model comparison —
then assess the fit.

- [Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and
  adirmc](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/estimator-comparison.md):
- [Advanced
  usage](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/advanced.md):
- [Diagnostic
  plots](https://leidenpharmacology.github.io/admixr2/articles/diagnostic-plots.md):
